reviews.review_text:
[ HEADS UP: Save at the start of and after completing each mission. It's an old game that's prone to the occasional crash. ]

I played this game as a teen, so it was nice coming back and being able to beat it on Normal instead of Easy this time. I've always loved this game, even with its mediocre optimization. It still holds up, which is more than I can say for those 6 countries and 5 soldiers I lost.


If you're going to buy this as a first-time XCOM player, I strongly suggest purchasing Enemy Within along with the base game. It's not overwhelming, and it deeply enriches the experience. If you like it, move on to XCOM 2. It's even harder, in my opinion.

非常好的游戏，但我不推荐Take-Two Interactive旗下任何一款游戏。
Really good game but I do not recommend any game published by Take-Two Interactive.

2025年2月28日，Take-Two更新了旗下所有游戏的EULA。2025年6月。
On February 28, 2025, Take-Two updated its EULA for all titles.

新协议条款包括：收集设备标识符、IP地址、精准地理位置、支付信息（含信用卡/借记卡号）、网站和应用浏览记录、账户凭证，并获取系统根目录访问权限；美国及澳大利亚、瑞士、英国、欧洲经济区以外地区的用户须放弃集体诉讼和陪审团审判权利，一切争议通过有约束力的个人仲裁解决；禁止使用修改器、MOD、漏洞利用，禁止使用VPN、代理服务器、虚拟机伪装位置或居住地。协议适用于Take-Two旗下所有游戏。
The updated terms include: collection of device identifiers, IP addresses, precise geolocation, payment information (including credit/debit card numbers), web and app browsing history, account credentials, and root-level access to the system directory; users in the U.S. and regions outside Australia, Switzerland, the U.K., and the EEA must waive class action and jury trial rights, resolving all disputes through binding individual arbitration; and the prohibition of mods, cheats, exploits, VPNs, proxies, and virtual machines used to disguise location or residence. These terms apply to all Take-Two games.

我只推荐你在无法获取盗版的情况下购买。
I only recommend purchasing if you cannot obtain a pirated copy.
Playing in 2026 and loving it. Definitely holds up. The sound of aliens getting blasted never gets old.

The best compliment I can pay this game is I happily restarted it four times before ever beating the campaign. This was my first real dive into the tactical turn based genre so I really didn't know what I was doing and kept screwing up. This game is so fun and so addictive so I was fine starting over until I felt comfortable with the games systems. Perhaps the game is poorly tutorialized but I think that really falls on me and no tutorial can really compare to just playing the game until you just teach yourself through exposure. 
     
     While I was playing this I also started a couple fire emblem games (shadow dragon and six) and I came to the conclusion xcom really does this genre a lot better for two key reasons. Firstly the gameplay and specifically the levels and terrain systems are just way deeper and more complex than what fire emblem was doing. I get those games are older and on handheld systems but still as gameplay experiences theres just so much more to sink your teeth into and it stays interesting for the games whole duration.

     Secondly is story priority xcom wisely keeps the story light and out of the way. The premise is all thats really needed for player motivation and the permadeath and elements of chance within missions creates stories far more compelling than heaps of dialogue and cutscenes ever could. Fire emblem is chock full of dry and long winded visual novel conversations that kill the pacing and never provide the stakes present within the missions themselves. Seeing your soldiers grow mission by mission going from poorly armored rookies with terrible aim to plasma wielding super soldiers creates attachment to characters effortlessly and shows the importance of gameplay prioritization and how its creates real emotion without any need for traditional characterization. 

     Surprisingly I ended up liking the base management stuff more than i expected. Its not the most in depth thing but it requires enough thought and progresses so nicely that the out of mission experience wasnt a drag. Your monthly budget is almost never enough to cover all the upgrades and additions you want so theres always a decent amount of compelling choices to make even in your base. The ufo fights are a real sore spot though, its pretty brainless and theres no choices to make besides make sure you linearly progress forward in the upgrade tree. The soldier equipment kind of had a similar problem in that progression is as simple as buy and and equip the gear with bigger numbers. Making kinetic and energy weapons more effective against certain enemy types would be a simple change that would keep older gear meaningful instead of just something you eventually throw away.

     The expansion enemy within was not at all what i expected. It functions as more of a pretty in depth new game plus where its the exact same campaign with many new gear options, a second faction, and new map and mission types. You could just play enemy within and not miss anything but it throws a lot more at you much quicker so the base game served an important role as a long introduction to the game and all your experience makes the expansion a lot smoother to play. Part of the dlcs are a wider variety of cosmetic options and while having no gameplay effect at all theming and dressing up your soldiers was lowkey one of my favorite parts of the game and gets you even more attached to the little guys. 

     This game certainly has tons of replayability but I definitely gotta play the others first. Its a phenomenal game i'd recommend to just about anyone and goes to show just how good turn based combat can be and how valuable it is to have no barrier between gameplay and story. It ran surprisingly well for a 14 year old pc game but there will be some jank and bugs so just make sure to save before and after every mission.
i played this to death on the xbox 360. love this game and recommend it to anyone who likes tactical RPGs like fire emblem

This is one of the best turn based strategy games I have ever played. XCOM is one of my top games of all time.

The base mechanic is truly enjoyable and the pacing of the game feels natural, I also really enjoy the multiplayer part of the game

I would suggest this game to anyone that enjoys strategy games
Best turn based tactical fighting game ever, if only the love put into making this was in other games in the series.

Widdawy genre-defining. Is that not the highest level of any piece of art, that it revives a dead genre and spawns dozens of imitators?
Incredible game with a fun story, good mechanics and deep replayability

An excellent tactics game! So XCOM Enemy Unknown is a very important game in the sense that it revived the tactics RPG sub-genre and that almost every modern TRPG and even some CRPGs took it’s gameplay and did their own version of it. I originally played this game on PS3 and enjoyed it a lot even though it can be a very punishing game at times. So I wanted to replay and actually finally finish it as I hadn’t before. So what did I think?

Visually, I still think the game looks pretty decent all things considered. The environments look good and the general art direction has let this game age pretty well. The aliens still look very good and detailed. The humans look okay but I mentioned in my review of Marvel’s Midnight Suns how the humans always look damn ugly and like they're straight out of The Sims 3. It still has some of that here but I’d argue this game looks better then the Marvel one.

There’s the base management which is fairly simple as you have your 2 departments of research and engineering. As you develop new tech in research you can then build it in engineering. This definitely had an addictive quality to it as you unlock new stuff constantly. Especially when you perform autopsies on dead aliens as well as capturing and interrogating them. Then there’s managing the council of countries that fund the XCOM project and their panic level being a failstate mechanic that can end your game if too many countries leave. I also love that my own country of South Africa was included in the council and that you can recruit soldiers from there. Though the ironic part was South Africa was the first country to leave the council in my game which definitely annoyed me lol.

Combat is probably the most influential part that many other games took from, as you move your soldiers in a 2 point action economy. I really loved the tactical element as you have to make use of cover and flanking maneuvers to get a one up on the aliens and don’t advance your soldiers too far in because the aliens will all tag team on one guy. There is also the XCOM meme of your guys having a chance to hit depending on how close they are to the target. And the fact that you could have a 90% chance to hit and still miss. I honestly have a love hate relationship with it as while it does play into the strategy of combat, it’s also extremely luck based and prone to some terrible RNG. Like you can go a whole turn of your guys missing every single shot but then on the aliens turn they hit your guys dead on like 99% of the time. Overall, I like the combat but the RNG nature leaves a lot to be desired.

One area I didn’t like though was the intercept missions where you have to send one of your fighter jets to take down an UFO so you can then send a squad in to assault it afterwards. Now while you can build new weapons and one time use abilities for your jets, you only get 2 planes in the whole game being the normal human jet and alien hybrid jet that you get later on. I like the management aspect though where you have to launch satellites over countries on different continents and then ship planes to them so that when a UFO shows up you're prepared. However, the ship combat is really simple with you just having this low res digitised version of the fight and there’s not much you can do. And the game will punish and chew you out if you don’t do these missions even when it's like a massive alien ship vs one of your tiny jets with no real way of you winning.

Finally there’s the story which is decent for the most part. While this is obviously a game that focuses mainly on the gameplay side the game does make an effort to have cool cinematic cutscenes with your 3 department heads acting as the main characters in them. I still think they could have done a bit more to have a compelling narrative like for example one mission had a whole cutscene of me finding this woman who had been captured by the aliens and had developed psychic abilities. And the cutscene was cool looking like something straight out of an X-Men film. But afterwards you never get to talk or even recruit this girl and all of her backstory is told to you by the department heads in passing while you manage your base. Still I do appreciate the effort they went into with it all. Also it's worth noting that the one possible ending where you lose the game is actually the canon ending that sets up XCOM 2 which is pretty interesting.

But overall, a good game and one I really enjoyed replaying. It has aspects that I critiqued but I still think its a solid time and one I can recommend.

LWR mod is good and must play, if you like ♥♥♥♥ and ball torture. I love it, like lego yoda once said, "Crush my ♥♥♥♥ with a rock I must, maximum pain I must endure, okay here I go"

XCOM in a nutshell
-Base under attack 
-Have a unit of 9 soldiers 
-Put all of them on overwatch to trap enemy
-1 alien sectoid runs in front of all 9 soldiers with less then 4 square spaces gap
-All 9 fire on the alien sectoid
-All 9 miss
-Rage quick and place lips on gun barrel to end it all
10/10 would play again

This was my introduction to the XCOM franchise and a great game.  Also, I'm pretty certain the playtime listed for the game on Steam isn't right.  I know I did at least one Long War playthrough. . .  Also, I watched a lot of gameplay of other people playing this game back in the day, so even if I hadn't played it at all I'd still have gotten a lot out of the game.  Anyway, this a 9/10 for me.

I love this game but the steam version annoyed the heck out of me with RANDOMLY failing to launch enemy within AND launching enemy unknown in stead so I can't continue my run.

And it's been an issue happening on and off for a decade now for people based on me looking it up. 

I had it running, I'm 30 hours in, then it decides to randomly stop working and start launchin genemy unknown.  What? 

Don't bother here, go buy the DRM free gog version for your own sanity like I should have done.
Incredibly well put together game here. The vibe is culmination of the best pre 2010 tropes and truly encapsulates how amazing the gaming space used to feel back then.

This might just be one of the best turn-based strategy games I've ever played. Don't let my hours fool you, I have PLENTY of hours on the PS3 version of this game. Trying out the Long War mod for the first time, I expect great things.

Quel bonheur ce jeu, pur génie tactique,
où chaque décision devient presque dramatique,
des aliens surgissent dans la nuit métallique,
et ton sang reste froid malgré le chaos critique.

Tu planifies, tu doutes, chaque tour est décisif,
un soldat tombe, et ton cœur devient pensif,
mais tu relances encore, toujours plus offensif,
car ce jeu rend l’esprit vif et combatif.

Les missions s’enchaînent en tension fantastique,
la victoire se mérite, jamais automatique,
et chaque réussite a un goût épique,
dans ce chef-d’œuvre stratégique et magnifique.

"A Banger" as the kids say. 
Classic turn-based alien killing, base managing & squad controlling fun. 
Definitely recommend getting the DLC. It fits seamlessly into the main game from the start.

I have been playing xcom since the floppy disk era.  This modern reboot is very different but keeps true to the spirit of the game and adds new elements such as glamcam that make for exciting engaging replay after replay.
Very good game similar to Civ, but instead managing an Alien fighting force and defending the planet. Game may seem hard at first but you will soon spend hours managing and strategizing.

Pros: Psionics are useful without being broken like they were in the original games. Managing your economy is a constant challenge, You can't just  sell alien tech willy nilly

Cons: advancement feels a bit linear compared to the original,
Game won't start anymore. Tried some common solutions, they don't work. Maybe I have to check more, but if you sell a game, you should make it work without having me lose time looking for solutions.
I've played enough of this game that the coders may have a legit lawsuit to help pay for their kids to go to college.  It's freakin awesome.

The game is extremely buggy, it constantly freezes, crashes, or softlocks, and the gameplay is incredibly frustrating.

The challenge of XCOM is managing risk. Hedging your bets, and making sure the infamous "Missed 99% shot" doesn't result in a game over. In practice, this doesn't matter, because the REAL challenge is how well you can game the enemy "pod" activation. Enemies don't do anything until they enter your line of sight, so instead of trying to keep eyes on all the important chokepoints or get a clear view of things for your sniper, you want to instead make yourself as blind as humanly possible to mitigate the chance of activating multiple pods. If you get dogpiled, you are probably going to wipe. So gouge out your eyes and minimize the amount of map you can actually see.

The gameplay itself is also extremely jank. Entire sections of wall can be removed by a missed pistol shot, leaving you exposed to instant death from a flanking attack. Overwatch sometimes just doesn't work. Getting wiped because my sniper refused to take a pistol overwatch shot against a 1 health chrysallid coming around the corner is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. I put them there to handle that exact thing from happening. The glitches and jank decided my strategizing meant nothing. If you're playing EW, be aware that throwing a battle scanner and revealing a pod of seekers before activating them with your troops will result in a softlock. The ability I explicitly took to deal with seekers actually just makes the game literally unplayable. INCREDIBLE. Nothing I do matters because the game is a janky, glitch filled mess and no amount of risk management or strategizing or positioning will ever fix that.

And then there's the freezing. If I let the game finish the cutscene for the skyranger landing back at base, the game freezes my computer. I have verified file integrity, uninstalled and reinstalled the game, updated my drivers, and every other thing I could think of and it still happens.

Overall, while the game is fun when it isn't bugging out, its almost never not bugging out. Because of this, I cannot recommend this game.
